title: Data Visualizations
subtitle: Interactive D3 projects during Cornell
summary: I attended this interesting course INFO 5100 at Cornell, where we were taught the principals of data visualizations and how to use d3.js to realize those ideas. There were three course projects that are very interesting.

blocks.text: The first project was a static course chart showing the course distributions of three adjacent majors in Cornell over a week, where we can see the temporal and spatial concentrations of courses.
The second project was an interactive kitchen board called "Online Kitchen", where you can drag your food and see the nutritions of the combined meal.
The third project was a composite interactive storyboard showing the history, relationship and popularities of major programming languages.

sections.label: Cornell Fall 2021 Class of ECE, CS & INFO

blocks.text: This is a data visualization course project realized with D3.js, check the visualization outcome in web page [here](https://greenere.github.io/visualizations/cornell_class/cornell_class_ece_cs_info_fall2021.html)
It is a course project in collaboration with Han Xia, Yingxin Lin and Yuhao Lu.
This is a data visualization course project realized with D3.js, check the visualization outcome in web page [here](https://greenere.github.io/visualizations/online_kitchen/online_kitchen.html).
It is a course project in collaboration with Yuhao Lu, Yue Wang and Yue Zhao.

This is a data visualization course project realized with D3.js, check the visualization outcome in web page [here](https://greenere.github.io/visualizations/programming_languages/programming_languages.html).
It is a course project in collaboration with Yuhao Lu, Yue Wang and Yue Zhao.
The project aims to display some interesting facts about programming languages in terms of their histories and popularities.
